summary: This indicator tracks returns to shelter among adult families who exited without a housing subsidy — a very small group, with DHS reporting only around 100-140 such exits citywide in a typical year. With so few cases, a handful of additional families returning to shelter can swing the percentage by several points, which is the most likely explanation for the sharp jump in the FY26 year-to-date figure rather than a meaningful change in outcomes.

sources.label: DHS, Preliminary Mayor's Management Report, Fiscal 2026
